summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a classic early‑1969 show, highlighted by a soaring New Potato Caboose bass solo and a monster Dark Star.
review: The Miller‑remastered soundboard captures the band with a spacious, clear stage feel; occasional vocal mix quirks at the very start are outweighed by overall superb clarity. Musically the night shines – Phil Lesh’s extended New Potato Caboose solo, an intense The Other One, a textbook Dark Star and tight renditions of Dupree’s Diamond Blues and Doin’ That Rag make this one of the most memorable 1969 performances.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 5‑star praise, highlighting standout songs and a high‑quality soundboard; only minor mix issues are noted, resulting in an overall rating well above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller’s remaster provides excellent soundstage and clarity. A brief vocal‑mix dip around 1:11 and some volume shifts are mentioned, but overall the instruments and ambience are crisp and well balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens with an unusually long tuning intro, then rolls through Cryptical Envelopment into The Other One and a fiery New Potato Caboose. The second set kicks off with a focused 20‑minute Dark Star followed by classic mid‑tempo numbers, delivering a balanced mix of exploratory jams and tight songcraft.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s Les Paul tone is praised; his vocals are low in the early mix but come forward later. Strong playing on Dark Star, Dupree’s Diamond Blues and Doin’ That Rag, with a brief missed vocal cue noted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s extended New Potato Caboose solo is described as “fabulous” and uniquely prominent; his bass is clear and leads the band through The Other One and other jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s vocals are initially undermixed but improve later; he joyously launches into Dupree’s Diamond Blues and adds harmony on several tracks.
band_member_comments.Keys: Tom Constanten’s delicate, nuanced keyboards are highlighted as a perfect complement throughout the set, especially during The Other One and Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart is noted for using maracas on Dark Star; both Mickey and Bill (referred to as “Billy”) receive praise for exceptional drumming, particularly in the drum segment.
